sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system documentation available for NES shoot-em-up

special_mechanics.name: Flight
special_mechanics.description: Player character flies through the air in eight directions with automatic screen scrolling

physics_values.shooting_constraint: can only shoot left or right regardless of flight direction

special_mechanics.name: Satellite Modules
special_mechanics.description: Two orbiting satellite modules that shoot simultaneously with player weapon. Can orbit (one above, one below) or be locked in player-selected position via A button

special_mechanics.name: Speed Power-up
special_mechanics.description: Collectible power-up that increases player movement speed

tier_notes: Tier 2 empirical/qualitative data. S.C.A.T. is a side-view shoot-em-up featuring free flight mechanics rather than traditional platformer physics. No precise movement values, gravity constants, or sub-pixel data available in documentation. Game mechanics focused on flight direction, weapon orientation, and satellite positioning rather than jump/gravity physics typical of platformers.

version_notes.difficulty_differences: Japanese version (Final Mission) is harder with 3 starting hit points and weapon loss on any hit; US/EU versions start with 6 hit points and only lose weapons on game over
version_notes.satellite_mechanics_us_eu: satellites aligned by player left/right movement

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - S.C.A.T.: Special Cybernetic Attack Team
Wikipedia - S.C.A.T.: Special Cybernetic Attack Team
